preamble: The transformation from a voiced aspirated velar plosive (/gʱ/) to a voiced pharyngeal fricative (/ʕ/) is a complex phonetic shift that typically occurs through a series of lenition processes. This involves the loss of aspiration, spirantization from a plosive to a fricative, and a backing or debuccalization of the articulation from the velar to the pharyngeal region.

examples.note: A documented substitution or variation where the voiced velar fricative (/ɣ/), a common lenited form of a velar stop, shifts to a voiced pharyngeal fricative (/ʕ/).

sources: Al-Jubouri, A. M. A., & Al-Jubouri, A. A. A. (2024). Substitution in Arabic: Lisan Al-Arab Dictionary as a Model. Theory and Practice in Language Studies, 14(1), 116-122.
